finding: An AI worker could edit files outside its task scope before ATM commit/claim gates noticed. Gemini 0258 produced broad dirty WIP and formatter spillover beyond the intended keep-list, and ATM only surfaced the problem after the shared worktree was already polluted.
expectedBehavior: ATM should provide a write-ticket or editor adapter pre-write guard that checks the current task allowedFiles before filesystem mutation, with post-write fallback evidence for tools that cannot intercept writes. Normal out-of-scope needs should route through governed scope amendment, not silent dirty spillover.
evidenceOrRepro: Plan 3.1 Gemini 0258 counterexample: worktree included out-of-scope/formatter changes such as actor-registry, identity, bootstrap, command-manifest, identity-normalization, and commit-bundle-assembly while the intended task focused on dirty-release/WIP recovery.
followUp: Prioritize ATM-GOV-0259 write-ticket/scope amendment guard. Require adapter pre-write checks, post-write violation records, actor mismatch diagnostics, and no second permission model.
